name: rust-github-actions
description: Rust-specific CI patterns: clippy, cargo-nextest, cargo-deny, sanitizers, coverage, and release automation

decisions.decision: Separate CI jobs for formatting, linting, testing, and security scanning with dtolnay/rust-toolchain
decisions.rationale: Separate jobs provide fast feedback on the specific failure type. dtolnay/rust-toolchain is the maintained replacement for the deprecated actions-rs toolchain. Parallel jobs reduce total CI time.
decisions.details:
Jobs:
1. fmt — cargo fmt --all -- --check
2. clippy — cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features (with RUSTFLAGS="-Dwarnings")
3. test — cargo nextest run --workspace
4. deny — cargo deny check (advisories, licenses, bans, sources)
5. coverage — cargo llvm-cov (on main branch only)

Use dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable (or @nightly, @1.XX for MSRV). Use Swatinem/rust-cache@v2 after toolchain setup for CARGO_HOME and target/ caching.

decisions.decision: Test on stable + MSRV; optionally nightly with continue-on-error; cross-platform matrix for binaries
decisions.rationale: Stable catches current issues. MSRV ensures downstream compatibility. Nightly previews upcoming breakage but should not block PRs. Cross-platform testing catches platform-specific bugs.
decisions.details:
Matrix strategy:
  rust: [stable, "1.XX"] (MSRV from rust-version in Cargo.toml)
  os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest] (for binaries)

Nightly job: continue-on-error: true.

fail-fast: false to ensure all combinations complete.

For cross-compilation (ARM, musl), use cross-rs via houseabsolute/actions-rust-cross action.

Test feature combinations with cargo-hack: cargo hack test --feature-powerset --depth 2.

decisions.decision: Use Swatinem/rust-cache for Cargo and target directory caching
decisions.rationale: Swatinem/rust-cache automatically caches ~/.cargo and target/ directories, keyed on Cargo.lock, toolchain, and job. It sets CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 for reproducible CI builds. Saves 2-5 minutes per job.
decisions.details:
Place after toolchain setup, before build steps. Key options: shared-key for cross-job sharing, cache-on-failure: true to cache even on test failures, save-if for conditional saving.

For workspaces with multiple Cargo.lock files, the action auto-detects. Cap at 10 GiB per repository (GitHub Actions limit).

decisions.decision: Run cargo-deny in CI on every PR; cargo audit on weekly schedule
decisions.rationale: cargo-deny checks advisories, licenses, bans, and sources in one pass. Running on every PR catches issues at introduction time. Weekly cargo audit catches newly disclosed vulnerabilities in existing dependencies.
decisions.details:
Use EmbarkStudios/cargo-deny-action@v2 with matrix strategy for parallel checks:
  matrix:
    checks: [advisories, bans, licenses, sources]
  steps:
    - uses: EmbarkStudios/cargo-deny-action@v2
      with:
        command: check ${{ matrix.checks }}

Requires deny.toml configuration in the repo. Start permissive, tighten over time.

Weekly cron job runs cargo audit separately as a safety net.

decisions.decision: Run sanitizers in CI: ASan+UBSan together, TSan separately, optionally MSan
decisions.rationale: Sanitizers catch memory safety and concurrency bugs that tests alone miss. ASan and TSan are mutually exclusive and must run as separate jobs. MSan is Clang-only and requires all dependencies instrumented.
decisions.details:
Job 1: RUSTFLAGS="-Zsanitizer=address" cargo +nightly nextest run (ASan + UBSan)
Job 2: RUSTFLAGS="-Zsanitizer=thread" cargo +nightly nextest run (TSan, for multithreaded code)

Sanitizers require nightly Rust. Use -Zbuild-std to build stdlib with sanitizer instrumentation. Set ASAN_OPTIONS=detect_leaks=1 and UBSAN_OPTIONS=print_stacktrace=1.

Run Miri as an additional CI job: cargo +nightly miri test (detects UB that sanitizers miss).

decisions.decision: Use cargo-llvm-cov for code coverage; upload to Codecov
decisions.rationale: cargo-llvm-cov uses LLVM source-based instrumentation for accurate, region-level coverage. It is cross-platform (Linux/macOS/Windows), unlike cargo-tarpaulin which is Linux-only and ptrace-based.
decisions.details:
Install: cargo install cargo-llvm-cov
Run: cargo llvm-cov --workspace --lcov --output-path lcov.info
Upload: codecov/codecov-action@v4 with file: lcov.info

Target 80%+ coverage. Run on main branch merges to track trends. Optionally run on PRs with diff coverage reporting.

decisions.decision: Use release-plz for version bumping and changelog; cargo-dist for binary packaging and GitHub Releases
decisions.rationale: release-plz automates version bumping from Conventional Commits, changelog generation via git-cliff, and crates.io publishing. cargo-dist handles cross-platform binary builds, installers, and GitHub Release uploads. They complement each other.
decisions.details:
release-plz: Runs on push to main, creates a release PR with version bumps and changelog updates. On merge, publishes to crates.io.

cargo-dist: Triggered on tag push, builds binaries for all targets, creates GitHub Release with installers (shell script, PowerShell, Homebrew tap).

For simpler projects, cargo-release handles both versioning and publishing in one tool.
